message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  During a COVID-19 outbreak in a prison in Zambia from 14
                  <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                  to 19
                  <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                  December 2021, a case control study was done to measure vaccine effectiveness (VE) against infection and symptomatic infection, when the Omicron variant was the dominant circulating variant. Among 382 participants, 74.1% were fully vaccinated and the median time since full vaccination was 54 days. There were no hospitalizations or deaths. COVID-19 VE against any SARS-CoV-2 infection was 64.8% and VE against symptomatic SARS-CoV-2 infection was 72.9%. COVID-19 vaccination helped protect incarcerated persons against SARS-CoV-2 infection during an outbreak while Omicron was the dominant variant in Zambia.
                </jats:p>
title: COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness during a prison outbreak when the Omicron was the dominant circulating variant— Zambia, December 2021
